constructors.name: Vector4
constructors.summary: Constructs a default-initialized [Vector4] with all components set to [code]0[/code].

constructors.name: Vector4
constructors.summary: Constructs a [Vector4] as a copy of the given [Vector4].

constructors.name: Vector4
constructors.summary: Constructs a new [Vector4] from the given [Vector4i].

constructors.name: Vector4
constructors.signature: public Vector4(float x, float y, float z, float w)
constructors.summary: Returns a [Vector4] with the given components.

members.name: Abs
members.summary: Returns a new vector with all components in absolute values (i.e. positive).

members.name: Ceil
members.summary: Returns a new vector with all components rounded up (towards positive infinity).

members.name: Clamp
members.signature: public Vector4 Clamp(Vector4 min, Vector4 max)
members.summary: Returns a new vector with all components clamped between the components of [param min] and [param max], by running [method @GlobalScope.clamp] on each component.

members.name: Clampf
members.signature: public Vector4 Clampf(float min, float max)
members.summary: Returns a new vector with all components clamped between [param min] and [param max], by running [method @GlobalScope.clamp] on each component.

members.name: CubicInterpolate
members.signature: public Vector4 CubicInterpolate(Vector4 b, Vector4 preA, Vector4 postB, float weight)
members.summary: Performs a cubic interpolation between this vector and [param b] using [param pre_a] and [param post_b] as handles, and returns the result at position [param weight]. [param weight] is on the range of 0.0 to 1.0, representing the amount of interpolation.

members.name: CubicInterpolateInTime
members.signature: public Vector4 CubicInterpolateInTime(Vector4 b, Vector4 preA, Vector4 postB, float weight, float bT, float preAT, float postBT)
members.summary: Performs a cubic interpolation between this vector and [param b] using [param pre_a] and [param post_b] as handles, and returns the result at position [param weight]. [param weight] is on the range of 0.0 to 1.0, representing the amount of interpolation. It can perform smoother interpolation than [method cubic_interpolate] by the time values.

members.name: DirectionTo
members.summary: Returns the normalized vector pointing from this vector to [param to]. This is equivalent to using [code](b - a).normalized()[/code].

members.name: DistanceSquaredTo
members.summary: Returns the squared [url=https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Euclidean_distance]Euclidean distance[/url] between this vector and [param to]. This method runs faster than [method distance_to], so prefer it if you need to compare vectors or need the squared distance for some formula.

members.name: DistanceTo
members.summary: Returns the [url=https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Euclidean_distance]Euclidean distance[/url] between this vector and [param to].

members.name: Dot
members.summary: Returns the dot product of this vector and [param with].

members.name: Floor
members.summary: Returns a new vector with all components rounded down (towards negative infinity).

members.name: Inverse
members.summary: Returns the inverse of the vector. This is the same as [code]Vector4(1.0 / v.x, 1.0 / v.y, 1.0 / v.z, 1.0 / v.w)[/code].

members.name: IsEqualApprox
members.summary: Returns [code]true[/code] if this vector and [param to] are approximately equal, by running [method @GlobalScope.is_equal_approx] on each component.

members.name: IsFinite
members.summary: Returns [code]true[/code] if this vector is finite, by calling [method @GlobalScope.is_finite] on each component.

members.name: IsNormalized
members.summary: Returns [code]true[/code] if the vector is normalized, i.e. its length is approximately equal to 1.

members.name: IsZeroApprox
members.summary: Returns [code]true[/code] if this vector's values are approximately zero, by running [method @GlobalScope.is_zero_approx] on each component. This method is faster than using [method is_equal_approx] with one value as a zero vector.

members.name: Length
members.summary: Returns the length (magnitude) of this vector.

members.name: LengthSquared
members.summary: Returns the squared length (squared magnitude) of this vector. This method runs faster than [method length], so prefer it if you need to compare vectors or need the squared distance for some formula.

members.name: Lerp
members.signature: public Vector4 Lerp(Vector4 to, float weight)
members.summary: Returns the result of the linear interpolation between this vector and [param to] by amount [param weight]. [param weight] is on the range of [code]0.0[/code] to [code]1.0[/code], representing the amount of interpolation.

members.name: Max
members.summary: Returns the component-wise maximum of this and [param with], equivalent to [code]Vector4(maxf(x, with.x), maxf(y, with.y), maxf(z, with.z), maxf(w, with.w))[/code].

members.name: MaxAxisIndex
members.summary: Returns the axis of the vector's highest value. See [code]AXIS_*[/code] constants. If all components are equal, this method returns [constant AXIS_X].

members.name: Maxf
members.summary: Returns the component-wise maximum of this and [param with], equivalent to [code]Vector4(maxf(x, with), maxf(y, with), maxf(z, with), maxf(w, with))[/code].

members.name: Min
members.summary: Returns the component-wise minimum of this and [param with], equivalent to [code]Vector4(minf(x, with.x), minf(y, with.y), minf(z, with.z), minf(w, with.w))[/code].

members.name: MinAxisIndex
members.summary: Returns the axis of the vector's lowest value. See [code]AXIS_*[/code] constants. If all components are equal, this method returns [constant AXIS_W].

members.name: Minf
members.summary: Returns the component-wise minimum of this and [param with], equivalent to [code]Vector4(minf(x, with), minf(y, with), minf(z, with), minf(w, with))[/code].

members.name: Normalized
members.summary: Returns the result of scaling the vector to unit length. Equivalent to [code]v / v.length()[/code]. Returns [code](0, 0, 0, 0)[/code] if [code]v.length() == 0[/code]. See also [method is_normalized]. [b]Note:[/b] This function may return incorrect values if the input vector length is near zero.

members.name: Posmod
members.summary: Returns a vector composed of the [method @GlobalScope.fposmod] of this vector's components and [param mod].

members.name: Posmodv
members.summary: Returns a vector composed of the [method @GlobalScope.fposmod] of this vector's components and [param modv]'s components.

members.name: Round
members.summary: Returns a new vector with all components rounded to the nearest integer, with halfway cases rounded away from zero.

members.name: Sign
members.summary: Returns a new vector with each component set to [code]1.0[/code] if it's positive, [code]-1.0[/code] if it's negative, and [code]0.0[/code] if it's zero. The result is identical to calling [method @GlobalScope.sign] on each component.

members.name: Snapped
members.summary: Returns a new vector with each component snapped to the nearest multiple of the corresponding component in [param step]. This can also be used to round the components to an arbitrary number of decimals.

members.name: Snappedf
members.summary: Returns a new vector with each component snapped to the nearest multiple of [param step]. This can also be used to round the components to an arbitrary number of decimals.

members.name: W
members.summary: The vector's W component. Also accessible by using the index position [code][3][/code].

members.name: X
members.summary: The vector's X component. Also accessible by using the index position [code][0][/code].

members.name: Y
members.summary: The vector's Y component. Also accessible by using the index position [code][1][/code].

members.name: Z
members.summary: The vector's Z component. Also accessible by using the index position [code][2][/code].

values.name: AXIS_W
values.summary: Enumerated value for the W axis. Returned by [method max_axis_index] and [method min_axis_index].

values.name: AXIS_X
values.summary: Enumerated value for the X axis. Returned by [method max_axis_index] and [method min_axis_index].

values.name: AXIS_Y
values.summary: Enumerated value for the Y axis. Returned by [method max_axis_index] and [method min_axis_index].

values.name: AXIS_Z
values.summary: Enumerated value for the Z axis. Returned by [method max_axis_index] and [method min_axis_index].

constants.name: AXIS_X
constants.summary: Enumerated value for the X axis. Returned by [method max_axis_index] and [method min_axis_index].

constants.name: AXIS_Y
constants.summary: Enumerated value for the Y axis. Returned by [method max_axis_index] and [method min_axis_index].

constants.name: AXIS_Z
constants.summary: Enumerated value for the Z axis. Returned by [method max_axis_index] and [method min_axis_index].

constants.name: AXIS_W
constants.summary: Enumerated value for the W axis. Returned by [method max_axis_index] and [method min_axis_index].

constants.name: ZERO
constants.summary: Zero vector, a vector with all components set to [code]0[/code].

constants.name: ONE
constants.summary: One vector, a vector with all components set to [code]1[/code].

constants.name: INF
constants.summary: Infinity vector, a vector with all components set to [constant @GDScript.INF].

operators.name: operator !=
operators.summary: Returns [code]true[/code] if the vectors are not equal. [b]Note:[/b] Due to floating-point precision errors, consider using [method is_equal_approx] instead, which is more reliable. [b]Note:[/b] Vectors with [constant @GDScript.NAN] elements don't behave the same as other vectors. Therefore, the results from this operator may not be accurate if NaNs are included.

operators.name: operator *
operators.summary: Transforms (multiplies) the [Vector4] by the transpose of the given [Projection] matrix. For transforming by inverse of a projection [code]projection.inverse() * vector[/code] can be used instead. See [method Projection.inverse].

operators.name: operator *
operators.summary: Multiplies each component of the [Vector4] by the components of the given [Vector4]. [codeblock] print(Vector4(10, 20, 30, 40) * Vector4(3, 4, 5, 6)) # Prints (30.0, 80.0, 150.0, 240.0) [/codeblock]

operators.name: operator *
operators.summary: Multiplies each component of the [Vector4] by the given [float]. [codeblock] print(Vector4(10, 20, 30, 40) * 2) # Prints (20.0, 40.0, 60.0, 80.0) [/codeblock]

operators.name: operator *
operators.summary: Multiplies each component of the [Vector4] by the given [int].

operators.name: operator +
operators.summary: Adds each component of the [Vector4] by the components of the given [Vector4]. [codeblock] print(Vector4(10, 20, 30, 40) + Vector4(3, 4, 5, 6)) # Prints (13.0, 24.0, 35.0, 46.0) [/codeblock]

operators.name: operator -
operators.summary: Subtracts each component of the [Vector4] by the components of the given [Vector4]. [codeblock] print(Vector4(10, 20, 30, 40) - Vector4(3, 4, 5, 6)) # Prints (7.0, 16.0, 25.0, 34.0) [/codeblock]

operators.name: operator /
operators.summary: Divides each component of the [Vector4] by the components of the given [Vector4]. [codeblock] print(Vector4(10, 20, 30, 40) / Vector4(2, 5, 3, 4)) # Prints (5.0, 4.0, 10.0, 10.0) [/codeblock]

operators.name: operator /
operators.summary: Divides each component of the [Vector4] by the given [float]. [codeblock] print(Vector4(10, 20, 30, 40) / 2) # Prints (5.0, 10.0, 15.0, 20.0) [/codeblock]

operators.name: operator /
operators.summary: Divides each component of the [Vector4] by the given [int].

operators.name: operator <
operators.summary: Compares two [Vector4] vectors by first checking if the X value of the left vector is less than the X value of the [param right] vector. If the X values are exactly equal, then it repeats this check with the Y values of the two vectors, Z values of the two vectors, and then with the W values. This operator is useful for sorting vectors. [b]Note:[/b] Vectors with [constant @GDScript.NAN] elements don't behave the same as other vectors. Therefore, the results from this operator may not be accurate if NaNs are included.

operators.name: operator <=
operators.summary: Compares two [Vector4] vectors by first checking if the X value of the left vector is less than or equal to the X value of the [param right] vector. If the X values are exactly equal, then it repeats this check with the Y values of the two vectors, Z values of the two vectors, and then with the W values. This operator is useful for sorting vectors. [b]Note:[/b] Vectors with [constant @GDScript.NAN] elements don't behave the same as other vectors. Therefore, the results from this operator may not be accurate if NaNs are included.

operators.name: operator ==
operators.summary: Returns [code]true[/code] if the vectors are exactly equal. [b]Note:[/b] Due to floating-point precision errors, consider using [method is_equal_approx] instead, which is more reliable. [b]Note:[/b] Vectors with [constant @GDScript.NAN] elements don't behave the same as other vectors. Therefore, the results from this operator may not be accurate if NaNs are included.

operators.name: operator >
operators.summary: Compares two [Vector4] vectors by first checking if the X value of the left vector is greater than the X value of the [param right] vector. If the X values are exactly equal, then it repeats this check with the Y values of the two vectors, Z values of the two vectors, and then with the W values. This operator is useful for sorting vectors. [b]Note:[/b] Vectors with [constant @GDScript.NAN] elements don't behave the same as other vectors. Therefore, the results from this operator may not be accurate if NaNs are included.

operators.name: operator >=
operators.summary: Compares two [Vector4] vectors by first checking if the X value of the left vector is greater than or equal to the X value of the [param right] vector. If the X values are exactly equal, then it repeats this check with the Y values of the two vectors, Z values of the two vectors, and then with the W values. This operator is useful for sorting vectors. [b]Note:[/b] Vectors with [constant @GDScript.NAN] elements don't behave the same as other vectors. Therefore, the results from this operator may not be accurate if NaNs are included.

operators.name: operator []
operators.summary: Access vector components using their [param index]. [code]v[0][/code] is equivalent to [code]v.x[/code], [code]v[1][/code] is equivalent to [code]v.y[/code], [code]v[2][/code] is equivalent to [code]v.z[/code], and [code]v[3][/code] is equivalent to [code]v.w[/code].

operators.name: operator unary+
operators.summary: Returns the same value as if the [code]+[/code] was not there. Unary [code]+[/code] does nothing, but sometimes it can make your code more readable.

operators.name: operator unary-
operators.summary: Returns the negative value of the [Vector4]. This is the same as writing [code]Vector4(-v.x, -v.y, -v.z, -v.w)[/code]. This operation flips the direction of the vector while keeping the same magnitude. With floats, the number zero can be either positive or negative.
